functional_requirements.title: Create Order
functional_requirements.description: User creates an order by specifying user ID and list of items. System checks user existence, product stock, calculates total price, deducts stock, and creates order record with status pending.

functional_requirements.title: Pay Order
functional_requirements.description: User pays for an order. System checks order exists and status is pending, checks user balance, deducts balance, and updates order status to paid.

functional_requirements.title: Ship Order
functional_requirements.description: Ship an order. System checks order exists and status is paid, then updates status to shipped.

functional_requirements.title: Complete Order
functional_requirements.description: Complete an order. System checks order exists and status is shipped, then updates status to completed.

functional_requirements.title: Cancel Order
functional_requirements.description: Cancel an order. System checks order exists and status is pending or paid, restores product stock, refunds user balance if paid, and updates status to cancelled.

functional_requirements.title: List Orders
functional_requirements.description: List orders with optional filters by user and status. Displays order details including items, total price, status, and creation time.

functional_requirements.title: Get User Orders
functional_requirements.description: Get all orders for a specific user, including total spent and count of orders per status.

functional_requirements.title: List Products
functional_requirements.description: List products with current stock. Optionally filter low stock products.

non_functional_requirements.description: Balance and stock must be consistent across operations. Deductions and restorations must be atomic.

acceptance_criteria.description: Create order with valid user and sufficient stock succeeds, stock deducted, order created with status pending.
Create order with insufficient stock fails with error message.
Pay order with sufficient balance succeeds, balance deducted, order status becomes paid.
Pay order with insufficient balance fails with error message.
Cancel pending order restores stock and no refund.
Cancel paid order restores stock and refunds balance.
Order status transitions correctly: pending -> paid -> shipped -> completed.

storage.details: Three independent in-memory data sources: users, products, orders.

glossary.order: A record of a purchase transaction containing user, items, total price, status, and creation time.
glossary.pending: Initial order status after creation, awaiting payment.
glossary.balance: User's account balance used for payments.

input_spec.description: Input is provided via the manage_order function with two arguments: command (string) and order_data (dict). The command specifies the operation, and order_data contains parameters.

schema.command: string (one of: create_order, pay_order, ship_order, complete_order, cancel_order, list_orders, get_user_orders, list_products)

order_data.items: list of dicts (optional, each with product_id and quantity)

output_spec.description: Output is a JSON object with success flag, message, and optional data.
